$comment: Ch 2 — THE SPIN, scenario 1 (dead release). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Schema mirrors bb-1-baseline.json. NOTE: geometry is intentionally identical to bb-1-baseline (d=4.6, h_r=2.0, θ=52°, v=7.52) — the chapter shift is narrative (the rim now doesn't forgive), not numerical. Spin/no-spin simulated via lesson copy + visual treatment, not engine physics (engine has no Magnus model).

meta.title: NO ROTATION.
meta.subtitle: CH 2 — THE SPIN, DEAD RELEASE
meta.authorIntent: The dead-spin release. Identical geometry to bb-1-baseline so the user re-enters answers from memory: d=4.6, h_r=2.0, θ=52°, v=7.52 m/s. Verification: t = 4.6 / (7.52·cos 52°) = 0.992 s; y(0.992) = 2.0 + 7.52·sin(52°)·0.992 − ½·9.8·0.992² = 3.051 m — matches h_h (err 0.001 m). Pedagogical purpose: make the absence of spin visible. The reveal beat ties the math the user just solved to 'in real basketball, this shot needs backspin to forgive a 5 cm miss — without it, this margin disappears.' Lesson copy carries the chapter's lens.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. A flat dead ball dies in front.
subheadVariants.2: Still short. Push the arc up.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. Dead release, no give.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Lift the arc.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Push the angle higher.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Nothing to soften the bounce.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long. Without rotation, the iron sent it away.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball. Dead release, dead arc.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the rim.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Same answer as the baseline free throw — by design. The chapter's twist isn't the math, it's the framing: a dead release with no backspin. Reveal copy: 'In real basketball, that 5 cm of margin disappears without spin.'

hints.body: Same equations as Ch 1. Spin isn't in the math — it's in the rim's reaction. Solve the trajectory.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
Ball at the rim when x = d and y = h_h.
Substitute t = d/(v·cos θ) into y. Try θ around 52°. Same answer as the baseline — see why the reveal says 'no margin'?

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 7.52 m/s.

intro.headline: NO ROTATION.
intro.subhead: The release is dead. The rim won't forgive that.

subheadByFlavor.SWISH: Threaded the eye. No rim to argue with.
subheadByFlavor.GLASS: Square caught it before the iron could.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Iron played fair. The shot survived.

miss.headline: RIM IRON

diagnosticByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Front iron. Dead release, no give.
diagnosticByCategory.BACK_RIM: Back iron. Nothing to soften the bounce.
diagnosticByCategory.SHORT: Came up short. A flat dead ball dies in front.
diagnosticByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Long. Without rotation, the iron sent it away.
diagnosticByCategory.AIRBALL: Air ball. Dead release, dead arc.

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: θ near 52°, push v near 7.5.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 52°, cut v by ~0.5.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 52°, push v near 7.5.
bracketHintByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Geometry: θ near 52°, cut v by ~1.0.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
